function_reachability: Is it impossible for function 'fread' to reach function 'fopen'?
Is it impossible for function 'fclose' to reach function 'fprintf'?
loop_reachability: Is it possible that the loop with condition 'i < n-1' is never executed?
Is the loop with condition 'i < n-1' always executed?
Is it possible that the loop with condition 'i < count' is never executed?
Is the loop with condition 'i < count' always executed?
dominators: Is it guaranteed that whenever line 2:  <statement> int main(int argc, char* argv[]) </statement> is reached, the statement line 13:  <statement> argv[0]); </statement> has already been reached?
Is it guaranteed that whenever line 10:  <statement> if(argc != 4) </statement> is reached, the statement line 8:  <statement> int count = 0; </statement> has already been reached?
data_dependency: Does the value of variable ofp at line 22: <statement>   ofp = fopen(argv[2], "w"); </statement> depend on the value of variable argv at line 2: <statement> int main(int argc, char* argv[]) </statement>?
Does variable argc at line 2: <statement> int main(int argc, char* argv[]) </statement> determine the value stored in variable i at line 37: <statement>     for(i=0; i < n-1; ++i) </statement>?
liveness: Does variable 'argc' remain in use at the end of function 'main'?
Does variable 'i' remain in use at the end of function 'main'?
dead_code: Can the statement 'if(i%10 == 9)' be considered dead code?
